classObjective: Find the coordinates of a missing endpoint when the midpoint and one endpoint are known.
workedExamples: 10-7, Example 4 — Find a Missing Endpoint When the Midpoint Is Known
10-7, Example 5 — Find a Missing Endpoint Algebraically
warmUp: If the midpoint of (2, y) and (6, 8) is (4, 5), find y
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: solving 2M_x - x_1 = x_2; midpoint equations with variables; algebraic approach to missing endpoints
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice finding missing endpoints using midpoint relationships
independentPractice: Practice problems finding missing endpoint coordinates when midpoint and one endpoint are known
assessment: Exit ticket: the midpoint of (x, 7) and (5, 3) is (3, 5) — find x
capReflection: Why does doubling the midpoint and subtracting the known endpoint give you the missing endpoint?
